englishDefinitions: A secondary show or attraction alongside the main event, especially at a carnival or circus.
Any activity or situation that is strange, distracting, or secondary to the main concern.
examples: At the old carnival, there was a sideshow featuring unusual acts that drew big crowds.
The courtroom drama became a sideshow, distracting from the real political issue.
The protesters said their concerns were being treated as a sideshow by the government.
